questions.stem:
One reasonable way to evaluate the of a scholar's research is to track how often other scholars refer to that
research. For example, Deutsche Bundesbank economist Claudia M. Buch, who studies banking regulations,
is among the world's most frequently cited researchers in her specialty, indicating that her work has had a
significant effect on the field. Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or
phrase?

Ethicist Ingrid Robeyns argues in her work that taxes on the very wealthy should be increased so that
governments will be enabled to solve problems of collective action, such as mitigating climate change.
Robeyns cites the work of economist Mariana shows that government subsidies are a more effective engine
of innovation than private sector investments; in addition, government-funded innovations tend to lead to
further developments in disparate fields (such as the United States mission to land a person on the Moon,
which accelerated developments in nutrition as well as navigation). Which choice best describes the overall
structure of the text?

choices.A:
It offers a claim by a scholar, then uses the results of a project by another scholar to show why that claim
should be rejected.
choices.B:
It summarizes the research of one scholar, then presents a study by another scholar that was inspired by
the findings of that research.
choices.C:
It details the findings of a research project by one scholar, then puts forward the suggestions of another
scholar for further investigation.
choices.D:
It introduces a proposition by a scholar, then describes that scholar's use of findings by another scholar to
support that proposition.

questions.stem:
The following text is from Angeline Boulley' s 2021 novel Firekeeper's Daughter. The narrator is an Ojibwe
teenager who runs in the morning before going to school. Levi is her younger brother. My brother
complains about my lengthy warm-up routine whenever he runs with me. I keep telling Levi that my longer,
bigger, and therefore vastly superior muscles require more intensive preparation for peak performance.
The real reason, which he would think is dorky, is that I recite the correct anatomical name for each muscle
as I stretch. ©2021 by Angeline Boulley What is the main topic of the text?

choices.A: The narrator's warm-up routine before running
choices.B: A high school teacher whose class the narrator enjoys
choices.C: The career that the narrator's brother hopes to pursue someday
choices.D: The narrator's feelings about moving to a new town

questions.stem:
Memoirs of Eleanor Eldridge is an 1838 historical account by Eleanor Eldridge and Frances Harriet Whipple
Green.(Take free, full-length more practice tests on the life of the book's subject beyond what is presented
in the text, writing, Which quotation from Memoirs of Eleanor Eldridge most effectively illustrates the
claim?

choices.A:
"Let us, dear reader, remember the punishment of idle curiosity, as taught in the true and affecting history
[named] 'Blue Beard' and, striving to be content with the facts in the case, seek not to lift the veil, which
the sensibility of true love, and feminine delicacy, have alike conspired to draw."
choices.B:
"Home is home, to the lowly as well as the great; and no rank, or color, destroys its sacred character, its
power over the mind, and the affections."
choices.C:
"Having thus so comfortably established ourselves, with no evil-minded eaves-dropper to make us afraid,
bend now, dear reader, thy most earnest and delicately adjusted ear, for I am going to tell thee a secret."
choices.D:
"How careful ought we to be to speak nothing but the truth, even in regard to the most trifling
circumstances; and not only so, but to be well assured that what we suppose to be true, is truth, before we
receive it as such."

questions.stem:
Through the process of photosynthesis, plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to create their own
food. This amazing ability allows plants and provides oxygen for other living things to breathe. Which
choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

In Puerto Rico, it's not unusual for a city or town to be known by a nickname that corresponds to one of its
notable features, like landscape, or chief exports. For example, the Puerto Rican municipality of San
Sebastian has also been called "the Cradle of Hammocks," a nickname that alludes to what the area is well
known for: the woven hammocks its residents famously produce. Which choice completes the text so that it
conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: @ The Carnegie Prize is an international
art competition. @ It is administered by the Carnegie Museum of Art in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. @ One
Carnegie Prize gold medal winner is Gaston La Touche of France. @ La Touche' s painting The Bath was
honored in 1907. The student wants to indicate the name of the artist who created the prize-winning
painting The Bath. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish
this goal?

choices.A:
The Carnegie Museum of Art in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, administers a competition honoring outstanding
artworks.
choices.B: In 1907, an artist from France won a Carnegie Prize gold medal.
choices.C: The Carnegie Prize is an international art competition administered by the Carnegie Museum of Art.
choices.D: The prize-winning painting The Bath is by the artist Gaston La Touche. wba

questions.stem:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes: @ A river's stream length is the length
of a line that follows along the river path. @ A river's valley length is the length of a straight line drawn
from the beginning of the river to the end. @ The river's curviness, or sinuosity, is the ratio of the river's
stream length to its valley length. ®@ This ratio is less than or equal to 1.5:1 in low-sinuosity rivers and
greater than
